correctness.notes: Theorem joint-sharp-and-sep-bound / joint-kernel representation: The proof uses E_Q[1{A=a} b(Y(a))|X=x]=E_Q[p_a(X,U)b(Y(a))|X=x], but Assumption msm only bounds p_a=Q(A=a|X,U) and does not state A is conditionally independent of the potential-outcome vector given (X,U). The reproduced identity contains the additional term E_Q[(1{A=a}-p_a(X,U)) b(Y(a))|X=x], which need not vanish.; Theorem onestep-limit / data-selected threshold step: Definition def:onestep minimizes over all eta in [y_L,y_U], but Assumption nuisance gives nuisance expansion only uniformly near eta_*. The proof uses the missing localization/global-control term sup_{eta outside N(eta_*)}|hat C_eta-C_eta| or an equivalent separation argument to conclude hat eta -> eta_*; without it, C_{hat eta}-C_{eta_*} need not be o_p(n^{-1/2}).

perItemFindings.label: correctness
perItemFindings.one_line: [blocking] Theorem joint-sharp-and-sep-bound / joint-kernel representation: The proof uses E_Q[1{A=a} b(Y(a))|X=x]=E_Q[p_a(X,U)b(Y(a))|X=x], but Assumption msm only bounds p_a=Q(A=a|X,U) and does not state A is conditionally independent of the potential-outcome vector given (X,U). The reproduced identity contains the additional term E_Q[(1{A=a}-p_a(X,U)) b(Y(a))|X=x], which need not vanish. → The class M_Gamma(P) is larger than the joint-kernel class H_Gamma(P). Endpoint equalities can fail; at Gamma=1 the note claims point identification, while residual dependence of A and unobserved potential outcomes given U can still leave nontrivial partial identification.

perItemFindings.label: onestep-limit
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Theorem onestep-limit / data-selected threshold step: Definition def:onestep minimizes over all eta in [y_L,y_U], but Assumption nuisance gives nuisance expansion only uniformly near eta_*. The proof uses the missing localization/global-control term sup_{eta outside N(eta_*)}|hat C_eta-C_eta| or an equivalent separation argument to conclude hat eta -> eta_*; without it, C_{hat eta}-C_{eta_*} need not be o_p(n^{-1/2}). → The asymptotic linear expansion for hat W_sep is not established under the stated assumptions; the estimator may select an uncontrolled threshold outside the local neighborhood.

verbatim_critique:
D0.5.1 (math referee) found load-bearing correctness defects — re-derive the flagged step(s):
[blocking, setup] Theorem joint-sharp-and-sep-bound / joint-kernel representation: The proof uses E_Q[1{A=a} b(Y(a))|X=x]=E_Q[p_a(X,U)b(Y(a))|X=x], but Assumption msm only bounds p_a=Q(A=a|X,U) and does not state A is conditionally independent of the potential-outcome vector given (X,U). The reproduced identity contains the additional term E_Q[(1{A=a}-p_a(X,U)) b(Y(a))|X=x], which need not vanish.. Fix: The class M_Gamma(P) is larger than the joint-kernel class H_Gamma(P). Endpoint equalities can fail; at Gamma=1 the note claims point identification, while residual dependence of A and unobserved potential outcomes given U can still leave nontrivial partial identification.
[serious, conjecture:onestep-limit] Theorem onestep-limit / data-selected threshold step: Definition def:onestep minimizes over all eta in [y_L,y_U], but Assumption nuisance gives nuisance expansion only uniformly near eta_*. The proof uses the missing localization/global-control term sup_{eta outside N(eta_*)}|hat C_eta-C_eta| or an equivalent separation argument to conclude hat eta -> eta_*; without it, C_{hat eta}-C_{eta_*} need not be o_p(n^{-1/2}).. Fix: The asymptotic linear expansion for hat W_sep is not established under the stated assumptions; the estimator may select an uncontrolled threshold outside the local neighborhood.

Referee notes: I reproduced the joint-kernel endpoint, separated outer bound, pointwise-band comparison, finite vertex calculation, LP envelope derivative, corrected score, threshold-selection step, and variance target. The LP/score term algebra matches, but the front-matter model omits the conditional-exchangeability condition needed for the joint-kernel representation, and the one-step theorem lacks localization/global uniform control for the selected threshold.
